description: Classical scale invariance at tree level: no dimensionful parameters in Lagrangian. Scale breaking purely radiative via CW mechanism.
affected_outputs: Entire FOPT computation, GW spectrum prediction
required_solution: Already verified by critic-agent. Remains controlled as long as higher-order operators do not reintroduce dimensionful parameters.

description: No fermionic dark matter in the dark sector. Only new particles are complex scalar Phi and gauge boson A'.
affected_outputs: Constraint analysis, Dark matter phenomenology
required_solution: Deliberate model choice. Adding fermions would change CW beta function and affect FOPT parameters.

description: Portal couplings (epsilon, lambda_HPhi) unspecified and 'assumed small' but not quantified.
affected_outputs: Constraint analysis (19/23 constraints), Thermalization, BBN/CMB, Delta N_eff
required_solution: Specify numerical bounds for epsilon and lambda_HPhi. Compute thermalization rate, decay widths, branching fractions. Most impactful missing information.

description: xi = T_dark/T_SM = 1 hardcoded. Dark and visible sectors fully thermalized at same temperature.
affected_outputs: FOPT parameters, GW spectrum, Delta N_eff, Reheating
required_solution: Li & Nath (2026, arXiv:2602.14324) show xi as dynamical variable changes GW by up to 4 orders of magnitude. xi=1 gives Delta N_eff~0.57 > Planck bound. Scan xi or implement synchronous evolution.

description: Portals simultaneously small for FOPT but large enough for thermalization (xi=1). Unresolved quantitative tension.
affected_outputs: Model viability, xi=1 assumption, Delta N_eff
required_solution: Quantify required epsilon for MeV-scale thermalization (>=1e-10). Check consistency with 'small for FOPT'. Compare with BBN lower bound eps > 1e-9 to 1e-8 from Coy et al. (2025).

description: No tree-level cross-quartic with SM Higgs. FOPT potential is purely dark-sector, with small lambda_HPhi portal.
affected_outputs: FOPT parameters, Effective potential shape
required_solution: Consistent with dark-sector-focused analysis. If lambda_HPhi were large, it would modify vacuum structure.

description: One-loop CW approximation with fixed-scale logs at chi=chi0 (vev), no RG improvement.
affected_outputs: FOPT parameters (alpha, beta/H, Tn, Tp, Treh), GW spectrum
required_solution: Christiansen et al. (2025, arXiv:2511.02910) show proper RG running essential. 4D HT+Daisy at mu=pi T matches 2-loop 3D EFT. Fixed-scale at chi=chi0 may differ quantitatively.

description: Daisy resummation via Arnold-Espinosa scheme (standard). No 3D EFT used.
affected_outputs: FOPT parameters, Finite-T effective potential
required_solution: Standard and widely used. 3D EFT methods more rigorous but consistent when RG scale = pi T (arXiv:2511.02910). Controlled for current precision.

description: High-temperature expansion (small chi/T) for thermal effective potential. Validity near limits for extreme supercooling.
affected_outputs: FOPT parameters for most supercooled points
required_solution: High-T expansion valid for m/T << 1. But for alpha~10^11-10^15, T~10^-6 GeV, m_A'/T~O(100-1000), expansion is near/far beyond validity. Low-T expansion or full numerical evaluation needed.

description: Gaussian approximation for false vacuum fraction / nucleation rate.
affected_outputs: beta/H value, Nucleation and percolation temperatures
required_solution: Standard for most transitions. For extreme supercooling, arXiv:1611.05853 shows exponential rate approximation can break down. Current beta/H~17-35 positive, suggesting validity. R* approach would cross-check.

description: beta/H parameterization for inverse duration. Assumes exponential nucleation regime.
affected_outputs: GW spectrum amplitude and peak frequency, Mean bubble separation
required_solution: For ultra-strong supercooling (alpha>>1), R* ~ (8pi)^(1/3)v_w/beta breaks down. beta/H can become negative (arXiv:2003.08892, arXiv:2602.14324). Proper R* = n_b^{-1/3} needed.

description: Single GW template (dbf from Lewicki et al. 2025). No template averaging or marginalization.
affected_outputs: GW h^2Omega(f) shape, Posterior inference, PTA scores
required_solution: dbf is most appropriate for alpha>>1. But single template introduces systematic uncertainty. Template-averaging (as done for SMBHB models) would capture this.

description: dbf template calibration range may be pushed for extreme alpha values (10^11-10^15).
affected_outputs: GW amplitude for extreme alpha, Posterior credible regions
required_solution: Most extreme alpha may exceed calibration range. Spectral shape (f^2 slope, high-f falloff) remains physically motivated but normalization has additional uncertainty.

description: Runaway bubble walls (v_w -> 1) assumed for all viable points.
affected_outputs: GW spectrum amplitude and peak frequency
required_solution: Supercooled PT with alpha>>1 expected runaway. But NLO friction (Bodeker-Moore) can limit velocity (arXiv:2501.15649). GW amplitude scales strongly with v_w.

description: Exact T_reh and its effect on GW peak not computed self-consistently. Fixed g*=10 for f_peak_est.
affected_outputs: GW peak frequency, Post-PT cosmology, Delta N_eff
required_solution: Fixed g*=10 introduces O(10-30%) uncertainty. Reheating dynamics (instantaneous vs gradual) can affect spectrum shape (arXiv:2603.11184). Not modeled.

description: CW-fixed scalar quartic lambda_Phi not scanned independently, determined by beta_lambda=3*g_D^4/(8*pi^2).
affected_outputs: FOPT benchmark scan coverage
required_solution: Correct by construction for CW mechanism. Quartic is not a free parameter. Controlled.

description: Model not novel (verdict from librarian). arXiv:2501.11619 studied same model class with RG-improved potential.
affected_outputs: Scientific interpretation, Novelty claims
required_solution: Well-documented with direct citations. Analysis is complementary. Controlled if report acknowledges literature.

description: PTArcade convergence: N_samples=1e5, ~5700 post-thinning samples. No R-hat or ESS reported.
affected_outputs: Posterior credible regions, MAP, Bayes factors
required_solution: 5700 samples may suffice for 2D posterior, but formal convergence diagnostics (R-hat<1.01, ESS>1000) not reported. Monte Carlo uncertainty unquantified.

description: Priors: log-uniform v [5e-4,50] GeV, uniform g_D [0.3,1.5]. Posteriors not hitting boundaries but prior width uneven.
affected_outputs: Marginal posterior distributions, Credible intervals
required_solution: Priors wide enough to not truncate. Log-uniform for v appropriate. Sensitivity check with different bounds would confirm robustness.

description: Kinetic mixing epsilon unspecified. All 5 directly testable A' constraints depend on epsilon.
affected_outputs: Model viability, Thermalization, Dark photon phenomenology
required_solution: Viable window: 10^-9 < epsilon < 10^-4 (from LHCb/BaBar/BBN). Cannot verify without epsilon value. Most critical constraint blocker.

description: Higgs portal lambda_HPhi unspecified. All rho constraints depend on sin^2(theta) proportional to lambda_HPhi.
affected_outputs: Model viability under rho constraints, BBN/CMB
required_solution: Cosmological beam dump (arXiv:1812.07585) excludes sin^2(theta) from 10^-11 to 10^-6. Only sin^2(theta)<10^-11 clearly viable.

description: BBN constraint on A' gives LOWER bound epsilon > 1e-9 to 1e-8, not upper bound. Often misunderstood.
affected_outputs: Applicable epsilon range, Model viability
required_solution: Coy et al. (2025, arXiv:2405.10792): decay before BBN requires epsilon > 1e-9. Combined with collider epsilon < 1e-4 gives viable window 1e-9 to 1e-4.

description: Visible vs invisible A' decay competition determines which collider constraints apply. Requires branching fraction computation.
affected_outputs: Applicability of LHCb, BaBar, NA62 constraints
required_solution: For g_D~0.5, invisible width to rho+A'* may dominate if epsilon small. BaBar invisible constraint then more relevant than LHCb muon search.

description: Delta N_eff ~ 0.57 with xi=1 and 4 dark dofs, exceeding Planck 95% CL bound (0.3).
affected_outputs: Model cosmological viability, BBN/CMB predictions
required_solution: Most serious uncontrolled uncertainty. Possible resolutions: supercooling dilution (needs computation), non-thermal dark sector (contradicts xi=1), decay to SM before BBN (requires specific couplings).

description: Dark scalar rho freeze-in yield and relic overproduction cannot be evaluated without lambda_HPhi.
affected_outputs: rho abundance at BBN/CMB, rho decay constraints
required_solution: For sin^2(theta)>10^-6: thermal freeze-out (overproduction, excluded). For 10^-12 to 10^-7: freeze-in (must decay before BBN). For <10^-11: safe.

description: Some rho constraints only partially cover m_rho=30-70 MeV range. PS191 covers 100-150 MeV. CMS h->SS covers >0.4 GeV.
affected_outputs: Constraint coverage completeness
required_solution: Lower m_rho range (30-50 MeV) has incomplete coverage. NA62/E949 K->pi+invisible provides complementary coverage.

description: 204/925 (22%) FOPT scan points failed numerically (mostly 'Tn invalid: nan') at small v and g_D.
affected_outputs: Parameter scan completeness, PTA region boundaries
required_solution: Failures cluster at small v (<0.02 GeV) and small g_D (<0.5). Likely genuinely non-viable regions, but boundary determined by numerical stability, not physical criterion.

description: Eternal inflation flagged in 41 scan points at higher g_D. Correctly excluded.
affected_outputs: PTA-compatible region boundary
required_solution: Correctly identified physical boundary. Quantum fluctuations dominate when expansion faster than transition. Controlled exclusion.

description: Athron et al. (2024, arXiv:2306.17239) challenge about false vacuum trapping for supercooled FOPTs not directly addressed.
affected_outputs: Confidence in FOPT completion, Percolation condition
required_solution: Conformal models circumvent this (S_E->0 as T->0, see arXiv:2501.11619). Backend includes percolation check. Dedicated analysis per arXiv:2501.15649 would provide stronger confidence.

description: GW source modeling (sound waves vs bubble collisions) debated for extreme supercooling.
affected_outputs: GW spectrum shape and amplitude
required_solution: dbf template with bulk flow is most appropriate. But literature (Caprini et al. 2024, etc.) uses different prescriptions. Comparison study would quantify systematic uncertainty.

description: Benchmark scan uses simple bin-counting score (how many of 14 NG15 bins inside violins), not full likelihood.
affected_outputs: Benchmark ranking, Best-fit identification
required_solution: Useful heuristic. All best-fit points score 14/14 (saturated). PTArcade full likelihood provides rigorous inference. Benchmark scan is coarse filter.

description: Bayes factor vs SMBHB model not computed. Standard figure of merit in PTA FOPT papers is missing.
affected_outputs: Quantitative model comparison, Evidence for FOPT
required_solution: arXiv:2501.11619 and arXiv:2502.19478 report Bayes factors. Without this, statistical preference for FOPT over SMBHB cannot be quantified.

description: PTArcade campaign used partial chains (~5700 post-burn-in samples), full 1e5 run may not have converged fully.
affected_outputs: Posterior credible regions, Robustness of MAP
required_solution: Partial chains may not have reached full convergence, especially in tails. Posterior should be considered preliminary.

description: No non-Gaussianity, polarization, or frequency-dependent speed of sound effects modeled.
affected_outputs: GW spectrum shape at high precision
required_solution: Subdominant for current analysis. Undetectable with current PTA sensitivity. Will be relevant for SKA-era datasets.

description: Interpolated g_star(T) from file used for SM particle content. Not explicitly tracked.
affected_outputs: FOPT thermal quantities, Hubble evolution
required_solution: Standard approach. Table covers MeV-GeV range. Controlled.

description: All 4 directly testable theory constraints satisfied: perturbativity, CW consistency, classical scale invariance.
affected_outputs: Model self-consistency
required_solution: No action needed. Model internally consistent at theory level.

description: MAP from PTArcade (v=1.07 GeV) vs benchmark BP1 (v=0.43 GeV) differ by factor ~2.5.
affected_outputs: Best-fit interpretation
required_solution: Discrepancy from different metrics (likelihood vs bin-counting). PTArcade likelihood more reliable. 1-sigma ranges capture this.

description: No backend modules for A'/rho decay widths, freeze-in yields, or dark sector thermal history. 8 missing calculations identified.
affected_outputs: All experimental constraints (19/23 blocked), Cosmological viability
required_solution: Constraint agent identified 8 missing calculations. Without these, no definitive experimental constraint evaluation possible. Single most impactful pipeline limitation.
